subject: feat(ui): adopt z-index scale; document not-applicable fleet patterns
body:
Parts A–D of the architectural decisions + z-index audit.

- Add `constants/zIndex.ts` with the glow-props standard 8-tier scale
  (base 0, sticky 20, sheet 30, backdrop 40, menu 50, modal 60, toast
  70, debug 80). Doc-comment maps each tier to the synctone surfaces
  that use it.
- Replace every runtime `zIndex: <literal>` with `Z.*`:
  - home.tsx pull-to-refresh indicator 100 → Z.sticky
  - Toast / InAppNotification 9999 → Z.toast
  - PWADebugBanner collapsed pill + expanded panel 9999 → Z.debug
  - +html.tsx noscript JS-disabled fallback 9999 → Z.debug (topmost
    pre-mount surface; no runtime competition)
- Remove the redundant `zIndex: 1` on ChatBackground's content layer:
  DOM sibling order already paints the content above the absolute
  background pattern, so the explicit stacking was noise.
- CLAUDE.md gains two sections:
  - "Not Applicable Patterns" recording evaluated-and-skipped
    fleet patterns with revisit conditions: BURGER_MENU (chat apps
    don't use burger nav — ChatHeaderMenu + SettingsModal cover
    contextual + global surfaces), DOWNLOAD_PDF (chat transcripts
    want structured export, not window.print() layout), EVENT_BUS
    (Zustand + React hooks cover all current cross-concern flows;
    realtime fan-out lives in DB triggers).
  - "Z-Index Scale" documenting the tiers, where each is used, and
    rules (including the `web/index.html` reference-only exception).

`web/index.html` retains its legacy `z-index: 9999` CSS but that
file is ignored by Expo Router's static export (per existing
CLAUDE.md note), so it has no runtime effect.

Tags: ui, conventions, fleet-alignment, z-index, architecture
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch
